trivia_bullets.text: Never Happened is a House Dimir card from Guilds of Ravnica (GRN, October 2018). The Dimir guild's mechanic in this set is Surveil — looking at top-of-library cards and optionally binning them to the graveyard — which mechanically expresses the guild's lore identity as information thieves and memory manipulators. Never Happened is a non-Surveil Dimir card that extends that identity into direct exile of spells from hand or graveyard. [Scryfall; WebSearch: House Dimir MTG Wiki]
sources: MTG Wiki: House Dimir via WebSearch

trivia_bullets.text: Flavor text: "Dimir assassins have the precision to slay a single memory." — no speaker attributed. The card is the only printing of this oracle text; it has never been reprinted as of Scryfall records. [Scryfall]

At 3 mana ({2}{B}), Never Happened is a flexible but costed-higher variant of hand disruption. It sees both hand and graveyard, giving it incidental graveyard hate. Scryfall lists it with an EDHREC rank of 25,009 — indicating low inclusion across Commander decks — consistent with 3-mana discard spells generally losing out to Thoughtseize (1 mana) and Inquisition of Kozilek (1 mana) in competitive formats. In Pauper and Pioneer (where it is legal), cheaper alternatives dominate. [Scryfall EDHREC rank; WebSearch: Never Happened card analysis]
sources: WebSearch: AppTrigger GRN Dimir card analysis 2018

trivia_bullets.text: Artist Aaron Miller illustrated this card. Never Happened has not been reprinted and appears only in the GRN set per Scryfall's prints search. [Scryfall]

related_cards.name: Thoughtseize
related_cards.note: The premier 1-mana hand-disruption reference point Never Happened is measured against

related_cards.name: Inquisition of Kozilek
related_cards.note: Second primary 1-mana discard benchmark in competitive formats

related_cards.name: Mnemonic Betrayal
related_cards.note: Same Dimir memory-theft flavor space, same set — takes from graveyards at scale

related_cards.name: Thief of Sanity
related_cards.note: Flagship Dimir Surveil creature of the same set; represents the 'steal opponents' things' Dimir design theme
